note: Mathlib/RingTheory/SimpleRing/Defs.lean:27 — 'A ring R is simple if it has only two two-sided ideals, ⊥ and ⊤' (class IsSimpleRing (R) [NonUnitalNonAssocRing R]); same defining ideal-theoretic condition as 'simple algebra' but stated for any ring, not requiring an Algebra/base-field structure, so it's a strict generalization.
verify_note: decl confirmed at Defs.lean:27 (class IsSimpleRing (R) [NonUnitalNonAssocRing R], quote matches verbatim); Wikidata Q1405695 label='simple algebra', P279 subclass-of Q2030545='algebra over a ring', confirming simple algebra = simple ring + algebra-over-a-ring structure, so IsSimpleRing (no algebra structure required) is a genuine strict generalization, not an exact match — match_kind and medium confidence are both honest.
